bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

I am targeting a very high utility because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s all-valueA offer is valid but extremely poor for my preferences, and their threat to walk is cheap talk unsupported by any concession pattern. I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my previous all-valueE bid while holding firm on the most important issues.

I am targeting very high utility because it is still extremely early (t=0.04) and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA extreme offer that gives me very low utility. Their messages are threats/urgency claims, but the actual bidding behavior shows firmness rather than a real compromise, so I will make only a small concession on my low-weight issues while holding my high-weight issues.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small additional concession to avoid deadlock. The opponent has barely conceded, moving only issueE from valueA to valueB, and their threats are cheap talk; I will concede first on my low-weight issues while holding firm on issueC/issueD/issueE.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early (t=0.08), so there is no need to accept the opponent’s extreme low-utility offers. The opponent has only made a small concession on issueE while using threats in messages, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and concede only on low-weight issues A/B while holding firm on high-weight issues C/D/E.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid around 0.90, conceding only on lower-weight issues while preserving my strongest interests on issueC, issueD, and issueE. The opponent has made only minimal concessions and their threats to walk are not credible at t=0.10, so I will not move close to their extreme offer yet, but I will avoid repeating my last bid by conceding issueA further.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still very early (t=0.12), so there is no reason to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency, but their actual bids show only minimal concession, so I will concede mainly on my low-weight issueA while holding firm on high-weight issues C, D, and E.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.86 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain low-utility to me despite small movement. Their messages are mostly threats and urgency claims, but the actual time is only 0.14 and their bidding shows only limited concession, so I will concede on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the important issues D and E.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to concede on the high-weight issues C, D, and E yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their threatening messages are cheap talk; their only real concessions have been small movements on D or E, not enough to justify a large concession from me. I am making a modest concession on low-weight issueB while holding firm on the issues that matter most to me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still early in the negotiation, but I am making a visible concession from my prior position to avoid deadlock. The opponent’s offers remain very unfavorable on most issues, though their latest bid concedes issueE to my best value; their repeated walk-away threats are cheap talk given the actual time fraction. I will hold firm on the most important issues D and E while conceding on lower-weight A/B and partially on C.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.76 because the negotiation is only at t=0.20, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility bids yet. The opponent’s actual offers have improved only slightly despite repeated walk-away threats, so I will concede another step on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issueD and issueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72, since it is only t=0.22 and there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their bids show gradual movement mainly on issueD/issueE; I will concede further on the lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the highest-weight issueD and issueE.

At t=0.24, I am still targeting a relatively strong utility around 0.66 because there is ample time left and the opponent’s latest offer is still very low for me. Their messages are mostly threats/urgency, but the actual offers show only limited concessions; I will concede fully on the lower/mid-weight issues A, B, and C while holding firm on the most important issues D and E.

I am targeting about 0.62 utility by conceding all low-weight issues and making a limited concession on issueD while preserving issueE, one of my highest-weight issues. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low offers yet; their messages are mostly threats, and their actual bids fluctuate with only modest value to me.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, conceding modestly because we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has shown only limited movement from their extreme opening. Their offers suggest some willingness to move on issueE and occasionally issueC/issueD, while the repeated threats in messages are cheap talk and not credible given the actual time fraction. I am holding more value on the higher-weight issueD while conceding issueE to valueD and issueC to valueB to explore a tradeoff they may accept.

I am targeting a mid-high utility around 0.56: still protecting the most important issues D/E, while making concessions on A, B, and C and moving D down to a level the opponent has previously signaled. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not accept their very low-utility offers, and their repeated “walk away” messages look like pressure tactics rather than credible new information. Their bidding shows limited but real movement on B/C/D/E, so I am combining some of those concessions into a more realistic counteroffer.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.54: below my last offer but still preserving value on the high-weight issueD while conceding on issueE and low-weight issueB. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s current 0.42-utility bid; their repeated threats are cheap talk, while their offers show gradual but limited concession, especially on issueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.56: enough concession from my earlier offers while still protecting the high-weight issues D and E. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low offers, but their recent bids show some willingness to move on issueE and occasionally issueD; I am ignoring the repeated walk-away threats as cheap talk and using the actual bid pattern instead.
